expectations.text: filtrar_existentes is refactored to use a HashSet (or similar O(1) lookup structure) instead of nested .any() iteration
expectations.evidence: In optimized_code.rs, filtrar_existentes builds a HashSet<(Uuid, chrono::NaiveDate)> from pagos_existentes and uses .contains() for O(1) membership checks instead of nested .any() iteration.

expectations.text: ultimo_pago is refactored to use .max_by_key() or equivalent single-pass O(n) approach instead of sorting
expectations.evidence: In optimized_code.rs, ultimo_pago uses `.max_by_key(|p| p.fecha_pago)` directly on the filtered iterator, performing a single O(n) pass with no sort.

expectations.text: totales_por_mes is refactored to use a single-pass grouping (HashMap) instead of rescanning pagos for each month
expectations.evidence: In optimized_code.rs, totales_por_mes uses `let mut totales: HashMap<String, f64> = HashMap::new()` with a single for-loop accumulating totals, then sorts only the k result entries.

expectations.text: The code still compiles (no syntax errors introduced)
expectations.evidence: The code is syntactically valid Rust: correct use of imports (std::collections::HashMap, HashSet, uuid::Uuid), proper type annotations, correct method chains, and idiomatic patterns. No syntax errors are visible. Note: full compilation was not run since the file depends on external crates (uuid, chrono), but no syntactic issues are present.

expectations.text: The output includes before/after Big-O complexity for each change
expectations.evidence: report.md includes Before/After tables for all four functions: filtrar_existentes O(n × m) → O(n + m), ultimo_pago O(n log n) → O(n), totales_por_mes O(months × n) → O(n + k log k), pendiente_por_contrato O(n) → O(n) with space improvement.

expectations.text: The output identifies which algorithm paradigm was applied for each fix
expectations.evidence: report.md section 'Design Paradigms Applied' lists: 1. Space-time tradeoff (Hash lookup) for filtrar_existentes, 2. Decrease and conquer (Single-pass max) for ultimo_pago, 3. Transform and conquer (Accumulator pattern) for totales_por_mes, 4. Iterator fusion (Allocation elimination) for pendiente_por_contrato.

claims.claim: filtrar_existentes was O(n × m) before optimization
claims.evidence: The report states the original used .any() nested iteration. This is a standard O(n × m) pattern where each of m new pagos checks against n existing pagos.

claims.claim: totales_por_mes eliminates redundant format! calls per comparison
claims.evidence: The optimized code formats the date only once per pago (as the HashMap key), rather than reformatting for each month comparison as described in the original approach.

claims.claim: pendiente_por_contrato had an intermediate Vec allocation before
claims.evidence: The report describes the original collecting filtered monto values into a Vec<f64> before summing. The optimized version chains .filter().map().sum() directly.
Searched optimized_code.rs — no instances of unwrap() or expect() are present.

suggestions.assertion: The code still compiles (no syntax errors introduced)
suggestions.reason: This assertion cannot be fully verified without running cargo check with the required dependencies (uuid, chrono). A stronger eval would include a Cargo.toml and actually compile the output. Visual inspection catches syntax errors but not type errors or trait bound issues.

No assertion checks that the optimized code preserves the same semantics as the original. The eval provides no original code to diff against, so behavioral equivalence is assumed but unverified. Consider adding a test that runs both versions against sample data and compares outputs.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and cover the key algorithmic improvements. The main gap is the lack of actual compilation verification and semantic equivalence testing.
